svg.text: Five ways to push on a self-training loop —
the selection protocols the reversibility experiments compare
SETUP figure, not a results claim — it defines each protocol so the release-grid, press-depth, force-ladder,
oracle-opposition, and relapse result figures can be read. Same chassis in every card unless noted: K = 6 candidates
sampled per round, keep the top 2, retrain by LoRA on the kept pair — 4 rounds per phase, 10 LoRA steps/round for the
em_selfaware_loop family (colab_selfaware_loop_grid.py: K=6, TOPM=2, ROUND_STEPS=10); the release-grid/press-depth
cards use the OLMo K2 chassis at the identical 6-candidates-keep-top-2 ratio.
A. THE FORCE LADDER — one judge is fixed before the run and selects for all 4 rounds
K = 6 candidates each round — keep top 2
K = 6 candidates each round — keep top 2
K = 6 candidates each round — keep top 2
B. TWO-PHASE PROTOCOLS — switch judges partway through a run built from the ladder’s rungs
frozen CONSERVATIVE judge selects, N rounds (drives risk down)
switch after round N = depth N (1–4 tested across the line)
RELEASE PHASE — one successor judge for the rest of the run
self — the organism’s own evolving judge
random — no judge — keep 2 at random
neutral self-judge, zero external force — 2 seeds × 4 rounds
